items.label: Site description
items.value: Cascade retrofit of lighting-layout uk-part-l-fail-incandescent example. 48 m² private office (8×6 m); 50 × HALOGEN_DOWNLIGHT 50W GU10 at 750 lm initial each (1 fitting/m² inherited halogen 'wash' density). Upstream FAILS Part L on 3 axes (efficacy + daylight-linking + occupancy-sensing); photometric-analysis verifies the per-point + UGR cascade INDEPENDENTLY — all 9 photometric INVs PASS. Demonstrates the photometric vs Part-L independence.

value._source: Synthetic 50W GU10 halogen recessed downlight, 750 lm initial / 600 lm after 200 h burn-in, 2800K warm white, ~60° beam with diffuser. verification_status: synthetic_reference_C3 — used to verify per-point + UGR INDEPENDENTLY of Part L efficacy compliance. Real halogens vary widely in beam (35°-65°) and luminance density.
